title: Implement durable channel storage and ephemeral isolation
purpose: Implements the core durable channel storage path: messages published to a durable channel are persisted to haematite via append before acknowledgement. This brief also enforces the ephemeral isolation guarantee — ephemeral channels hold no DurableStore reference and perform zero haematite operations. The durable/ephemeral split is a channel property set at creation time, immutable for the channel's lifetime (P1).
task: Build the durable channel storage module. Define the message envelope type carrying payload, causal context, timestamp, publisher identity, and optional idempotency key. Implement partition routing that derives a partition index from the channel's partition key function. Implement the durable append path: append(channel_id + partition, envelope, expected_seq) with per-partition monotonic sequence tracking. Enforce that ephemeral channels never hold a DurableStore reference — use the type system (separate types or an enum with no store field on the ephemeral variant) to prevent accidental persistence calls. Scope boundary: this brief handles message persistence and partitioning. It does not implement consumer cursors (DUR-003), dedup (DUR-004), or conversations (DUR-005).

requirements.title: Define MessageEnvelope with all required fields
requirements.spec: MessageEnvelope struct SHALL contain: payload (Vec<u8>), causal_context (Option<CausalContext> — a parent reference or vector clock entry), timestamp (u64, epoch millis), publisher_id (String), and idempotency_key (Option<String>). MessageEnvelope SHALL serialize to bytes deterministically for storage and SHALL deserialize from stored bytes losslessly. MessageEnvelope SHALL implement Debug and Clone. CausalContext SHALL be a separate type carrying an optional parent message ID and an optional vector clock entry.
acceptance: MessageEnvelope has fields: payload (Vec<u8>), causal_context (Option<CausalContext>), timestamp (u64), publisher_id (String), idempotency_key (Option<String>).
CausalContext has fields: parent_id (Option<String>), vector_clock_entry (Option<u64>).
MessageEnvelope round-trips through serialize/deserialize with byte-identical output.
Both types implement Debug and Clone.

requirements.title: Implement partition routing from partition key function
requirements.spec: WHEN a message is published to a partitioned durable channel, THE SYSTEM SHALL apply the channel's partition key function to the message to derive a partition index. The partition index SHALL be in the range [0, partition_count). WHEN no partition key function is configured (single-partition default), THE SYSTEM SHALL route all messages to partition 0. The partition key function SHALL be a caller-provided closure `Fn(&MessageEnvelope) -> u64` whose output is mapped to the partition range via modulo.
acceptance: A channel with partition_count=4 and key function returning 7 routes to partition 3 (7 % 4).
A channel with partition_count=1 always routes to partition 0 regardless of key function output.
A channel with no key function configured routes all messages to partition 0.
Partition index is always < partition_count for any key function output.

requirements.title: Track per-partition monotonic sequence numbers
requirements.spec: WHILE a durable channel is active, THE SYSTEM SHALL maintain a monotonic sequence counter per partition. The sequence counter SHALL be initialized from the last stored sequence number on channel recovery (or 0 for a new channel). Each successful append SHALL advance the counter by 1. The counter SHALL be used as expected_seq on the next append call to haematite.
acceptance: A new durable channel starts with sequence 0 for all partitions.
After appending 3 messages to partition 0, the next expected_seq is 3.
Sequence counters for different partitions are independent.
The sequence counter value matches the return value from DurableStore::append.

requirements.title: Implement durable append with sequence conflict detection
requirements.spec: WHEN a message is published to a durable channel, THE SYSTEM SHALL serialize the MessageEnvelope, compute the partition index, and call DurableStore::append(channel_id + partition, envelope_bytes, expected_seq). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT acknowledge the publish until append succeeds. IF DurableStore::append returns SequenceConflict, THE SYSTEM SHALL propagate the error to the publisher as DurabilityError::SequenceConflict — it SHALL NOT retry silently. The stream key for append SHALL be formatted as "{channel_id}:{partition_index}".
acceptance: A successful publish calls DurableStore::append before returning Ok.
The stream key passed to append is formatted as "channel_id:partition_index".
A SequenceConflict from DurableStore::append propagates as DurabilityError::SequenceConflict to the caller.
No retry logic exists in the append path.
The envelope is serialized to bytes before being passed to append.

requirements.title: Enforce ephemeral channel isolation via the type system
requirements.spec: Ephemeral channels SHALL NOT hold a reference to DurableStore. The type system SHALL prevent accidental persistence calls on ephemeral channels — either through separate types (DurableChannel vs EphemeralChannel) or an enum where the Ephemeral variant carries no store field. WHEN a channel is configured with DurabilityMode::Ephemeral, THE SYSTEM SHALL construct the ephemeral variant with zero haematite interaction. Ephemeral channels SHALL perform zero append, read, cas, or scan operations.
acceptance: An ephemeral channel type/variant has no DurableStore field — this is statically enforced, not a runtime check.
Constructing an ephemeral channel does not require a DurableStore reference.
There is no code path from an ephemeral channel to any DurableStore method.
A durable channel requires a DurableStore reference at construction.
EphemeralChannel (or equivalent) implements Debug.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement consumer cursor tracking or checkpointing — that is DUR-003.
SHALL NOT implement message replay or read-from-offset — that is DUR-003.
SHALL NOT implement dedup key caching or receipt storage — that is DUR-004.
SHALL NOT implement conversation event sourcing — that is DUR-005.
SHALL NOT implement crash recovery beyond initializing sequence counters — that is DUR-006.
SHALL NOT add retry logic to the append path — SequenceConflict is a caller-visible error.
verification: cargo check compiles the channel module with no errors.
grep -rn 'DurableStore' on the ephemeral channel type/variant returns no hits.
MessageEnvelope round-trip test passes (serialize then deserialize produces equal value).
